pi_items.source: CDC IIS Standard Vaccine Code Set (https://www2a.cdc.gov/vaccines/iis/iisstandards/vaccines.asp?rpt=cvx) confirms CVX 150 = Influenza, split virus, quadrivalent, preservative free

FDA and GSK official sources confirm GlaxoSmithKline manufactures FLUARIX QUADRIVALENT (https://www.fda.gov/vaccines-blood-biologics/vaccines/fluarix-quadrivalent), which is a quadrivalent influenza vaccine matching CVX 150 classification

This is synthetic test data. setup_task.sh (line 11) explicitly deletes any existing record with this lot number before task execution. The task is for an agent to CREATE this record (not query existing data). This is test data generated for benchmark purposes.

This is synthetic test data. Format (YYYY-MM-DD) is correct and the expiration date is reasonable for a vaccine (approximately 1 year from task metadata year 2026). However, this is not based on actual real-world vaccine lot data; it is test data created for the task benchmark.

This is synthetic test data. The quantity of 50 units is reasonable for vaccine inventory management, but it is not derived from real-world data. This is test data created for the interactive agent task.

privileged_info_summary: CVX code 150 correctly represents Influenza, split virus, quadrivalent, preservative free vaccine according to CDC IIS standards. GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) is verified as the manufacturer of FLUARIX QUADRIVALENT, a quadrivalent influenza vaccine. All other metadata (lot number FL-2026-QA, expiration date 2026-12-31, and quantity 50) are synthetic test data created for this interactive agent benchmark task. The task requires an agent to add this synthetic vaccine lot to a NOSH ChartingSystem database, not to work with real vaccine inventory data.
